mnemonics: McGraw Hill (M C G R A W H I L L) - My Cool Group Reveals Awesome Ways to Handle Identity and Logarithms. (A playful mnemonic to remember that McGraw Hill is the publisher and to associate the topic with key group concepts like Identity and operations.)
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that is associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
The order of a group is the number of elements in its set.
Abelian (or commutative) groups are groups where the operation is commutative (a*b = b*a for all elements a, b).
commonMistakes: Assuming all group operations are commutative; this is only true for Abelian groups.
Forgetting to check the closure property when determining if a set and operation form a group.
Confusing the identity element (e.g., 0 for addition) with the multiplicative identity (1) when working with number-based groups.
